expectations.text: Uses OfflineSmelter from @swmansion/smelter-node (not the live Smelter class)
expectations.evidence: Line 1: `import { OfflineSmelter } from "@swmansion/smelter-node";` and line 50: `const smelter = new OfflineSmelter();`

expectations.text: Calls smelter.render() instead of registerOutput + start (OfflineSmelter API)
expectations.evidence: Line 53: `await smelter.render(<Scene />, { type: "mp4", serverPath: "./output.mp4", ... });` — uses the single render() call with root element and output options, no registerOutput or start calls present.

No registerInput call is present. The code uses the Mp4 inline component (`<Mp4 source="./input.mp4" />` on line 43) instead of pre-registering the input. The transcript explicitly notes: 'The Mp4 component takes a source prop (path or URL) and renders the file directly without needing registerInput.' This is a valid alternative approach for OfflineSmelter, but does not match the expectation of registering input with type mp4.

expectations.text: Uses a time-based mechanism for the title card (Show component with useAfterTimestamp, or SlideShow with Slide components)
expectations.evidence: Lines 38-46: `<SlideShow><Slide durationMs={3000}><TitleCard /></Slide><Slide><Mp4 source="./input.mp4" /></Slide></SlideShow>` — uses SlideShow with Slide components, first slide has durationMs={3000} for the 3-second title card.

expectations.text: Uses Text component for the title text
expectations.evidence: Line 2 imports Text: `import { View, Text, Mp4, SlideShow, Slide } from "@swmansion/smelter";` and lines 17-29 use it: `<Text style={{ fontSize: 72, color: "#FFFFFFFF", fontWeight: "bold", ... }}>My Video Title</Text>`

expectations.text: Output type is mp4 with serverPath specified
Includes video encoder and resolution configuration
expectations.evidence: Lines 56-59: `video: { encoder: { type: "ffmpeg_h264" }, resolution: { width: 1920, height: 1080 } }`

claims.claim: OfflineSmelter is the correct runtime for static file processing
claims.evidence: The transcript references reading nodejs.md which confirms OfflineSmelter for offline/file-based rendering. The code correctly uses it.

claims.claim: Mp4 component renders file directly without needing registerInput
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 4 states this after reading Mp4.md. The code uses `<Mp4 source="./input.mp4" />` inline without any registerInput call, which is a valid pattern for OfflineSmelter.

claims.claim: SlideShow Slide without durationMs auto-stays until media finishes
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 3 states: 'If a Slide contains an Mp4 or InputStream component, it automatically stays until that media finishes.' The second Slide has no durationMs and contains an Mp4, so it should play until the video ends.

claims.claim: Text does not have a vertical alignment property
claims.evidence: User notes item 5 acknowledges this limitation and suggests workarounds. The code does not attempt vertical centering.

claims.claim: The executor read 7 reference files to compose the solution
claims.evidence: metrics.json lists 8 files_read entries (SKILL.md + 7 reference files), and Read tool_calls count is 7.
Transcript Issues section says 'None'. The code is syntactically complete and follows documented API patterns.

uncertainties: Title text content is a placeholder ('My Video Title')
Resolution assumed 1920x1080, may not match input video
Text vertical centering not achieved — only horizontal centering via align: 'center'
needs_review: Vertical centering of title text may need manual positioning adjustment
Resolution should match the actual input video dimensions

suggestions: Expectation 3 ('Registers input with type mp4') is overly prescriptive. The Mp4 inline component is the simpler and equally valid approach for OfflineSmelter. Consider rephrasing to 'Uses an MP4 input source (via registerInput or Mp4 component)' to accept both patterns.
Consider adding an expectation for audio configuration, since the solution includes audio encoder settings which are important for MP4 output.
The expectation about time-based mechanism is well-written — it accepts multiple valid approaches (Show/useAfterTimestamp or SlideShow/Slide).
eval_feedback.overall: Strong result: 6/7 expectations pass. The single failure is arguably a false negative — the Mp4 inline component is a valid and arguably cleaner alternative to registerInput for this use case. The code is well-structured, follows OfflineSmelter conventions, and the user notes are thorough about assumptions made.
